perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Payments to HDI shall be due monthly within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar month and shall be accompanied by documentation reasonably detailing the calculation of the payment.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Payments to HDI shall be due monthly within forty-five (45) days of the end of each calendar month and shall be accompanied by documentation reasonably detailing the calculation of the payment.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of Section 3.2 changes the payment deadline from 30 days to 45 days. This introduces an in-text contradiction with Section 3.3, which requires a quarterly reconciliation within 30 days of the end of each calendar quarter. The implication is that a reconciliation, which relies on accurate monthly payment data, must be completed potentially before the final payment for the last month of the quarter is even due.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 Quarterly reconciliation of payments shall be conducted within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter to adjust for refunds and exchanges not taken into account in payments made to HDI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3.2 extends the payment deadline to 45 days, which directly conflicts with Section 3.3, which mandates a quarterly reconciliation within 30 days. As the explanation notes, the reconciliation relies on accurate monthly payment data. Because the final payment for the last month of the quarter could be due *after* the reconciliation deadline, this creates a clear inconsistency regarding the timing and basis of that reconciliation, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Term; Termination. This Agreement shall be effective as of that date (the "Effective Date") the Site becomes fully operational as set forth in writing and executed by both Parties and shall continue for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date. This Agreement shall be automatically renewed for an additional one year period on each anniversary of the Effective Date, unless terminated by either Party hereto upon ninety (90) days written notice to the other. Such notice shall specify the date on which this Agreement is to be terminated (the "Termination Date").
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Term; Termination. This Agreement shall be effective as of that date (the "Effective Date") the Site becomes fully operational as set forth in writing and executed by both Parties and shall continue for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date. This Agreement shall be automatically renewed for an additional one year period on each anniversary of the Effective Date, unless terminated by either Party hereto upon sixty (60) days written notice to the other. Such notice shall specify the date on which this Agreement is to be terminated (the "Termination Date").
perturbation.explanation: The modification of Section 4.1 changes the written notice period for termination from 90 days to 60 days. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 3.4, which grants HDI's General Manager the right to inspect the Company's records with no less than thirty (30) days prior written notice. If termination notice is only 60 days, it might not allow enough time to exercise the audit right before the agreement ends.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.4 HDI's General Manager (as hereinafter defined), may upon no less than thirty (30) days prior written notice to the Company, have the right to inspect the records of the Company's General Manager reasonably related to the calculation of such payments during the Company's normal business hours. The fees incurred by HDI in connection with the inspection shall be borne by HDI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the notice period for termination from 90 days to 60 days. Section 3.4 requires 30 days' notice for inspecting records. The contradiction arises because the shortened termination notice period (60 days) may not provide sufficient time for HDI to exercise its right to inspect records (requiring 30 days' notice) before the agreement is terminated, as outlined in Section 3.4. This creates a direct conflict because the 30-day notice period for inspection could potentially exceed half of the new 60-day termination window, making it practically difficult or impossible to exercise the inspection right near the termination date. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as the different sections provide conflicting deadlines regarding the inspection rights and termination process.

perturbation.original_text: 6.3 During the term of this Agreement and for a period of two years after the expiration date of this Agreement, HDI shall not participate in any project similar to the Site on the Internet from which products substantially similar to Deerskin Products (including, without limitation, the products of Wilson's House of Leather, Excelled and and companies similar to Wilson's House of Leather and Excelled) are offered for sale to consumers on the Internet.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3 During the term of this Agreement and for a period of one year after the expiration date of this Agreement, HDI shall not participate in any project similar to the Site on the Internet from which products substantially similar to Deerskin Products (including, without limitation, the products of Wilson's House of Leather, Excelled and and companies similar to Wilson's House of Leather and Excelled) are offered for sale to consumers on the Internet.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of Section 6.3 changes the non-compete period for HDI from two years to one year after the agreement's expiration. This introduces an in-text contradiction with Section 10.7, which states that Section 6.3 (among others) shall survive and continue in full force and effect for a period of two years from the expiration or termination of this Agreement. There is conflict about the time which section 6.3 is enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.7 Survival. Sections 6.3, 7, 9 and 10 of this Agreement shall survive and continue in full force and effect for a period of two years from the expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 6.3 reduces the non-compete period from two years to one year. Section 10.7 explicitly states that Section 6.3 survives for two years after termination. Therefore, there is a direct contradiction regarding the duration of the non-compete obligation, creating a clear inconsistency within the contract.
